item.title: Shaping the Emerald City - NASA Science
item.description: The hills and basins of Seattle, Washington, have been sculpted by glaciers and modified by humans.

item.title: Viking: 50 Years on Mars — Poster - NASA Science
item.description: Throughout history, Mars had been in sight, but never within reach — until July 20, 1976, when NASA’s Viking 1 lander touched down safely and began

item.title: All CS2 maps explained
item.description: The Counter-Strike 2 (CS2) community has clear preferences for map picks in pro and casual play. The reasons often lie in the balance between two team roles and the variety of strategies.

item.title: T1 vs Gen.G and KC vs Dplus at LoL EWC 2026: Finals at the tournament of big upsets
item.description: This League of Legends (LoL) event treated us to quite a few spectacular upsets. Even the grand final participants were not actually the teams people expected to see battling for the trophy.

item.description: Here is every Jam Track in Fortnite Festival.

item.title: Nanoparticles could remove harmful immune molecules from blood
item.description: The immune system, the body's defense network against infections and injuries, can sometimes become too active. In these cases, it can produce too many immune mediators, fragments of genetic material or proteins that regulate immune responses.

item.title: Best CS2 crosshair codes & settings
item.description: Using crosshairs of pro players is a nice step towards competitive effectiveness. It is a good idea to experiment with different crosshairs and even adjust the settings for your personal playstyle and preferences.

item.title: Sun Gaming and Hospitality and The Sourcing Exchange Announce Strategic Partnership
item.description: Sun Gaming and Hospitality partners with The Sourcing Exchange to bring data-driven procurement, cost savings, and vendor transparency to gaming and hospitality.

item.title: Restarting Nuclear Power Plants: Preserving and Restoring Value - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:Recent growth in electricity demand, expanding carbon-free energy goals, and renewed concerns about energy security are driving utilities to reconsider nuclear power plants that have been permanently shut down or are approaching retirement. EPRI examines the technical, regulatory, and business factors that influence whether these facilities can return to service and what steps […]

item.description: NASA researchers recently put a new wing design, appearing long and thin with a lightweight structural design, through a series of grueling tests to find its

item.description: This composite of images taken by NASA’s Psyche mission shows the crescent of Mars grow as the spacecraft approached the planet for a gravity assist from May

item.description: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group to review updated VTE case information, additional data

item.title: The FGC has become too serious and we need more people like Hayao
item.description: The FGC has been real serious recently, and we need more whimsy, even if it comes in the form of a shocking tweet from Hayao.

item.title: NASA's Viking Mission
item.description: A gallery of images from NASA's Viking mission to Mars which, among its many "firsts" sent the first image from the surface of another planet back to Earth.

item.title: “Maybe it’s not actually voluntary": Are esports organizations pressuring VALORANT pros to compete in the EWC? Or does the issue go deeper than that?
item.description: Are VALORANT pros being pressured to compete in the EWC or risk losing their role on the team? A finance professional weighs in.

item.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby - NASA Science
item.description: Captured by the multispectral imager instrument on NASA’s Psyche mission, this is an enhanced-color mosaic created from four individual images acquired on May

item.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Delivers Mars Flyby Data, Time-lapse Video - NASA Science
item.description: When NASA’s Psyche spacecraft flew by Mars for a gravity assist on May 15, 2026, it gave the mission team an opportunity to test instruments and prep for science they’ll conduct when reaching the metal-rich asteroid Psyche in 2029, and collect images and a time-lapse video of the Mars encounter.

item.title: Thinner wires, faster electrons: Quantum material challenges copper at chip scale
item.description: Electrical interconnects may very well be the unsung heroes of modern microchips. These tiny wires—typically made of copper due to its high conductivity—string together the billions of transistors that drive our computers and electronic devices. But as the technology advances and additional transistors are piled on, the components must shrink to the nanoscale. And that's when copper begins to fail.

item.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
item.description: Lowest cost per token from extreme codesign maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training in the agentic era.

item.description: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the global coalition committed to responsible space exploration, becoming the newest signatory and seventh

item.description: More than 250 participants from around the world gathered to discuss advancing the transition to quantum-safe security.

item.title: NovoTags: AI-designed proteins help scientists see inside living cells | EMBL
item.description: Researchers have designed synthetic and inducible proteins that bind to bright fluorescent dyes with high specificity and affinity, significantly expanding the toolkit for multicolour imaging of proteins inside cells

item.title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau - NASA Science
item.description: The expansive mudflats, sandy beaches, and mangrove forests of the Bijagós archipelago support an array of migratory shorebirds and large numbers of sea turtles.

Part 2: Specifications of AKN 3.1 CS01 is now available for public review and comment before a call for consent as OASIS Standard. Akoma Ntoso defines an XML vocabulary for parliamentary, legislative, and judicial documents, enabling machine-readable exchange […]

item.title: Q&A: How Capcom Brought Path Tracing to RE ENGINE Across PRAGMATA and Resident Evil Requiem | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Capcom’s RE ENGINE team set out to bring path tracing into two shipping titles at once, Resident Evil Requiem and PRAGMATA, each with a different visual identity. Over two years, the RE ENGINE team…

item.title: Graphene nanoribbons survive gamma radiation, revealing potential sensors for fusion reactors
item.description: University of Arizona researchers have demonstrated a promising new application for graphene nanoribbons, a nanoscale semiconductor material with the potential to withstand extreme environments. The team's findings could help clear a key hurdle to bringing fusion energy to the electric grid.

item.title: Amazon will share its Q2 2026 earnings on July 30
item.description: On July 30, Amazon is set to hold a conference call to discuss its Q2 2026 financial results.

item.title: NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet - NASA
item.description: New research led by scientists at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California has revealed the identity of a puzzling near-Earth object by

item.title: Is Counter-Strike 2 going soft? Esports fans react to new comms rule and debate hate speech vs. trash talk
item.description: A newly-added rule states that what you say to your own teammates is no longer off-limits to TOs. Will this stop trash talking?

item.title: Brain-inspired nanopore device uses current-induced heating for memory operations
item.description: Some researchers are leaning into biology for inspiration in computing. In particular, neuromorphic computing offers a brain-inspired approach to hardware that replaces traditional binary processing with systems that function more like neurons and synapses. Now, a new study, published in Nature Communications, describes an innovative design for a fluidic memristor that uses its own self-heating mechanism to induce a history-dependent memory effect.

item.title: Hybrid material confirms antiferroelectricity can coexist with switchable polarization
item.description: Many of the advanced electronic components surrounding us in everyday life rely on polar materials to function. Polar materials have an uneven distribution of electric charge. This gives them a positive and a negative side even in the absence of an external electric field. The most important among these are ferroelectric materials, in which the direction of polarization can be reversed by applying an electric field.

item.title: Esports has an excellent opportunity to cash in on nostalgia right now
item.description: Nostalgia within the gaming industry is a surefire way for developers and publishers to generate huge profits.

item.title: Integrating Context-Aware Video AI Agents Into Enterprise Workflows | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: A video analytics AI agent that can perceive, reason, and act based on massive amounts of video footage must be integrated with existing workflows and applications to be useful.

item.title: Scaling Agentic AI Factories Through Extreme Co-Design with NVIDIA BlueField | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Agentic AI changes the infrastructure pattern for AI factories. One request can trigger many model calls, tool calls, memory lookups, policy checks, storage accesses, and network transfers before a…

item.title: A bioengineer shifts his career to explore how life experiences change the body
item.description: Shaped by a tumultuous childhood, Jason Buenrostro pivots from technology development to studying the biology of stress and adversity and finding new ways to boost resilience.

item.title: Cyclotron Road Welcomes 11 New Entrepreneurial Fellows
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) has announced the 2026 cohort of scientists and engineers selected to join Cyclotron Road at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (Berkeley Lab). Cyclotron Road is one of four nodes in the DOE Office of Technology Commercialization’s Lab-Embedded Entrepreneurship Program (LEEP) .

item.title: Mapping the Noncredit Data Taxonomy 3.0 to the CTDL: A New Benchmark Model for Noncredit Education Data | Credential Engine
item.description: A new recommended benchmark and publishing template from Credential Engine helps states, institutions, and agencies bring noncredit education data into the credential transparency ecosystem. Credential Engine has released a new...

item.title: Chemists shrink gallium nitride, the material behind LED lighting, into nanocrystals
item.description: Nanocrystals are so useful that they formed the basis of the 2023 Nobel Prize in Chemistry. But despite their usefulness, scientists have so far been able to make these microscopic crystals from only a limited palette of materials. A group of chemists at the University of Chicago and Argonne National Laboratory has announced a way to make nanocrystals from a useful class of materials known as metal nitrides—a previously impossible task.

item.title: Become a voice for global technology standards: launching the ETSI Ambassador programme - ETSI
item.description: More than 250 participants from around the world gathered to discuss advancing the transition to quantum-safe security.

item.title: Invitation to comment on KMIP Usage Guide v3.0 – ends 15 August 2026
item.description: OASIS and the KMIP TC are pleased to announce that the Key Management Interoperability Protocol Usage Guide Version 3.0 is now available for public review and comment.

item.title: Sharpen the Sword, Skip the Downloads — ‘Onimusha: Way of the Sword’ Is Coming to GeForce NOW
item.description: Plus, GeForce NOW launches in India moving from beta to public availability, and delivers ‘Denshattack!’ alongside five new games this week.

item.title: Why Indonesia’s Fisheries Future Hinges On Data Integrity and Trust
item.description: From vessel tracking to algorithmic enforcement, a surveillance revolution is transforming marine governance

item.title: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs approach to bioresilience
item.description: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs approach to bioresilience, using AI models to support prevention, detection and response.

item.title: Submer Group and Netceed partner to expand AI infrastructure across EMEA
item.description: Submer Group partners with Netceed to expand AI infrastructure capabilities across EMEA and support telecom and data center customers.

item.title: Cath Brooksbank: What I’ve learned | EMBL
item.description: Lessons on trusting your team and building a world-leading bioinformatics training programme from EMBL-EBI's Head of Training, Cath Brooksbank

item.title: Ontario Wildfire Smoke Moves East - NASA Science
item.description: Canadian wildfires sent plumes of smoke streaming over Ontario, Quebec, and parts of the U.S. Midwest and Northeast.

item.description: New NVIDIA Blackwell-powered T3000 and T2000 modules, paired with new NVIDIA Jetson software memory optimization and agent skills, help partners and customers move advanced robotics, visual AI and edge workloads onto compact, power-efficient systems.

item.title: Build a Multi-Camera 3D Tracking Application with NVIDIA DeepStream 9.1 Skills | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Developers building video analytics applications across large spaces must track the same object as it moves between camera views. Single-camera 2D tracking lacks reliable depth information and…

item.title: Advances in fine-tuning reduce total volume of AI training - Uptime Institute Blog
item.description: In the early days of generative AI, much attention was focused on the process of training new models from zero. As the technology matures, organizations are increasingly looking for ways to adapt existing large language models (LLMs) to their specific requirements. Customizing existing models avoids the need to source training data, employ machine learning experts […]
